prompt:
The workstation enrollment helper mostly works, but its idempotency check is too eager. An initial enrollment writes the expected simulated management record. Running the same command again is correctly a no-op, yet running it with a changed owner tag still reports `already enrolled` and leaves the old ownership metadata in place.

Repair `linuxenroll.sh` so the canonical requested owner tags participate in its desired-state fingerprint and reconciliation alongside the existing fields. Keep the interface and deterministic file formats intact. `enroll` must read host identity from `<root>/etc/hostname` and `<root>/etc/machine-id`, retain an absolute certificate reference whose file exists below the supplied root, record the fixed baseline packages, install the logging rule, and publish sorted owner tags both locally and in the simulated management-service host record. An identical rerun must not rewrite state or append an audit event; changing the requested owner tags must update state and append exactly one `updated` event. `unenroll` must remove all enrollment-owned state and the simulated host record while retaining the host identity, referenced certificate, and audit log, and a second unenrollment must be a no-op. Do not contact a network service, modify the real host, or change the protected tests.

Run the protected acceptance suite with `bash tests/test_linuxenroll.sh`.
